title: alnus incana subsp. rugosa
intro: Alnus incana subsp. rugosa, also known as the speckled alder or rough alder, is a subspecies of the alder tree that is native to North America. The bark and leaves of this plant have been traditionally used for their medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects. The health benefits of A. incana subsp. rugosa include its ability to reduce fever, soothe respiratory issues, and treat wounds. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and salves, and is also used in traditional medicine to treat a variety of ailments.
